description.en: Make an iterator that drops elements from the iterable while the predicate is true and afterwards returns every element. Roughly equivalent to: def dropwhile(predicate, iterable): # dropwhile(lambda x: x<5, [1,4,6,3,8]) → 6 3 8 iterator = iter(iterable) for x in iterator: if not predicate(x): yield x break for x in iterator: yield x Note this does not produce any output until the predicate first becomes false, so this itertool may have a lengthy start-up time.
